findings.description: reachable() never checks `seen` before enqueuing neighbors (line 31), so on any cyclic graph the queue grows without bound and the while-loop never terminates — an uncontrolled-resource-consumption / infinite-loop DoS (CWE-835/CWE-400) triggerable by untrusted graph input.

count_paths() recurses with no cycle guard despite only 'DAG assumed': a cyclic input causes infinite recursion -> RecursionError/stack-overflow, and even a valid deep or diamond-shaped DAG blows the recursion limit or explodes exponentially (no memoization) — an algorithmic-complexity DoS (CWE-674/CWE-407) reachable from untrusted data.

reachable() omits `start` from the returned set and, combined with the missing visited-check, returns incorrect reachability — a correctness flaw whose security relevance is that callers relying on reachability for access/dependency decisions could be misled; primary impact is the unbounded loop noted on line 28.

Node name is logged at INFO via %s; if node identifiers are attacker-controlled and logs are later rendered in a control-character-sensitive sink, this is a minor log-injection/forging consideration (CWE-117) — no format-string flaw since %s is used.
